headlines.title: Americans from hantavirus-hit cruise ship being monitored for observation, U.S. officials say
headlines.description: The 17 Americans who were aboard the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship Hondius have now arrived home in the United States

headlines.title: Americans evacuated from hantavirus-stricken cruise ship en route to the U.S.
headlines.description: The cruise ship at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak arrived early Sunday off the Spanish island of Tenerife, where the process of sending most passengers back to their home countries will get underway.

headlines.title: Florida surgeon who removed wrong organ says he is ‘forever traumatized’ by patient’s death
headlines.description: Thomas Shaknovsky said in a deposition in November that he believed he was removing 70-year-old William Bryan’s spleen, not his liver.

headlines.title: He pitched military service with a promise of lifetime healthcare. When he needed it, the system failed.
headlines.description: For more than a decade, Guy Shoemaker pitched military service to potential new recruits with one key promise: healthcare for life.“You’re going to have medical and dental for the rest of your life,” said Shoemaker, a retired Army sergeant first class and recruiter who spent a year in Afghanistan.

headlines.title: ‘A watershed moment’: A pancreatic cancer drug is set to transform treatment
headlines.description: An experimental pancreatic cancer drug that’s been shown to double survival in patients with advanced stages of the disease is poised to revolutionize the way the cancer is treated, oncologists say.

headlines.title: Breaking down health officials' update after Americans return to the U.S.
headlines.description: Health officials provided an update from Omaha, Nebraska, after most of the American passengers aboard the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship Hondius arrived there for medical treatment. NBC News medical contributor Dr. Kavita Patel breaks down the key takeaways from the media briefing, including how the patients are being cared for and level of risk to the general population.

headlines.title: Officials give update after Americans aboard hontavirus-hit cruise hit return to U.S.
headlines.description: Health officials provided an update from Omaha, Nebraska, after most of the American passengers aboard the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship Hondius arrived there for medical treatment. NBC News’ Daniele Hamamdjian reports on the steps being taken by health officials to ensure containment, as well as the status of the ship itself, which is anchored in Spain.

headlines.title: Neurologist Shares Simple Ways to Keep the Brain Healthy
headlines.description: Dr. Ayesha Sherzai, neurologist and co-author of “The Alzheimer's Solution” joins TODAY’s Jenna Bush Hager and Sheinelle Jones to break down the difference between brain fog and cognitive decline and shares simple ways to keep your brain healthy.

headlines.title: Inside Look at HRT and Why the Estrogen Patch is in Short Supply
headlines.description: Many women are currently facing struggles with getting access to proper menopause treatment so endocrinologist Dr. Rekha Kumar and author of “How to Menopause,” Tamsen Fadal, join TODAY to break down the latest on menopause treatment. They explain the importance of hormone replacement therapy, discuss why there is currently a shortage in estrogen patches, and highlight the benefits and misconceptions of menopause treatment.

headlines.title: The best running watches, tested over hundreds of miles
headlines.description: I’ve tested dozens of running watches from Garmin, Coros, Amazfit, Fitbit, Apple and more. Here are my top picks for beginners, races and ultra-athletes alike.

headlines.title: The best men’s walking shoes of 2026, tested and reviewed
headlines.description: Shop expert-recommended and staff-tested men’s walking shoes from Brooks, On, New Balance and Hoka.

headlines.title: I’ve tested dozens of workout headphones: These are the best
headlines.description: I tested over a dozen workout headphones from brands like Apple, Beats, and Shokz to find our top picks for running, biking, the gym and more.
